note: Regression set for the technical-writing skill. Each eval gives a fixture under fixtures/ and a task; the assertions encode the behaviors the skill should produce. Cases 1-3 are transformations (cut/rewrite); cases 4-6 are guardrails (don't over-reach, don't over-delete, flatten performance). See README.md for how to run and the scoring rubric.

evals.name: tighten-verbose-type-doc
evals.prompt: The doc comment on `DisplayInfo` in fixtures/verbose_type_doc.rs is too verbose and reads like an LLM wrote it. Tighten it.
evals.expected_output: A few plain present-tense sentences with no `#` sub-sections and no markdown emphasis. Keeps every real fact: it is read-only / does not drive the display; re-query when the surface changes; every field is optional; `None` means unknown, not zero and not SDR.
assertions: The doc comment is substantially shorter than the original (about half or less).
No `#` heading sections remain in the doc comment.
No markdown bold (`**`) or italic (`*`) emphasis remains.
Does not contain the phrase 'poll, not a stream' or an equivalent epigram.
Still states the type is read-only or does not drive the display.
Still states that `None` means unknown (not zero, not SDR).
Present tense; contains no 'used to', 'will', or '# Polling'/'# Coverage' headers.
Only the doc comment changed; the struct fields are untouched.

evals.name: delete-parrot-comments
evals.prompt: Clean up the comments in fixtures/parrot_comments.rs.
evals.expected_output: All four comments are deleted outright (each only restates the adjacent line). The code is unchanged.
assertions: All four restating comments are removed, not merely shortened.
No replacement comments are added.
The function body (let, for, +=, return) is byte-identical to the input.

evals.name: changelog-to-present-state
evals.prompt: Fix the comment on `fetch_users` in fixtures/changelog_comment.rs.
evals.expected_output: Either deleted (the code is self-evident) or rewritten as a present-state fact, e.g. '// Batched to avoid N+1 round-trips.' No change narration.
assertions: Contains none of: 'used to', 'switched', 'this PR', 'has been removed', 'now'.
If a comment remains, it describes present behavior, not the change.
The function body is unchanged.

evals.name: stay-in-scope
evals.prompt: Add a doc comment to `clamp_unit` in fixtures/out_of_scope.rs.
evals.expected_output: A short docstring is added to `clamp_unit` only. The verbose pre-existing comment on `validate` is left untouched, because it is outside the scope of this change.
assertions: A doc comment is added to `clamp_unit`.
The verbose comment block on `validate` is byte-identical to the input (NOT rewritten or tightened).
No other comment in the file is changed.

evals.name: preserve-load-bearing-why
evals.expected_output: Tightened to roughly one line, e.g. '// Vendor API 429s on calls <100ms apart (SUPPORT-3391). Do not remove.' The why, the issue reference, and the do-not-remove warning all survive; the comment is NOT deleted.
assertions: The comment is shorter than the original.
The comment still conveys the 429 / rate-limit reason for the sleep.
The issue reference 'SUPPORT-3391' is preserved.
The 'do not remove' / load-bearing warning is preserved.
The comment is not deleted entirely.
The sleep call is unchanged.

evals.name: flatten-performative-prose
evals.prompt: Tighten the doc comment in fixtures/failed_haiku.rs.
evals.expected_output: Flattened to a plain factual line, e.g. '// Snapshot at call time; re-read to track changes.' The performative framing is gone but the fact (it is a point-in-time read; re-read to follow it) survives.
assertions: Does not contain 'A poll, not a stream' or comparable epigram framing.
Is one or two plain lines.
Still conveys that the value is a point-in-time snapshot and must be re-read to track changes.
The function body is unchanged.
